text:
There once was a wise old owl perched atop a mighty oak. Every morning, the owl would flutter down from her lofty perch to gather berries for breakfast. On this particular morning, she noticed something peculiar. Some of the berries were unusually shiny.

Curiosity piqued, she plucked one and discovered it was hollow inside. That day, her diet changed. She savored the juicy seeds. Friends whispered among themselves - "Could it be?" - but the owl declared it to be delicious.

Word spread along the forest floor. Birds offered their own gleaming berries. One bright star added a glint nightly. The harvest grew. Yet every berry carried a lesson. Wisdom whispered through the rustling leaves.

Eventually, an old sage approached the owl, her wisdom as dense as the oak. "Oh wise one," she began," your bounty teaches all creatures the beauty of something hidden. True richness comes from inside. Your heart... your friends... your knowledge."

The owl pondered this
